name: Garfield County Bank
description_short: Locally-owned Montana bank serving Garfield County since 1960, offering full-service banking with two branch locations and FDIC insurance protection.
description_long:
Garfield County Bank has been a community fixture in Montana for over 60 years, establishing itself as a trusted local financial institution. Founded in 1960, the bank operates as a locally-owned enterprise deeply rooted in Garfield County and the surrounding region. The bank maintains its primary location at 444 Main Street in Jordan, Montana, with a full-service branch called Redwater Valley Bank in Circle, Montana, ensuring accessibility across the county.

The bank offers comprehensive full-service banking products and services typical of community banks, including checking and savings accounts, credit cards, mortgages, personal loans, and mobile banking capabilities. They provide modern digital conveniences such as a mobile banking app with mobile deposit functionality for both iPhone and Android users. The institution maintains ATM access at both locations—a drive-up ATM in Circle and a walk-up ATM in Jordan—plus night depository services. Customers can also access online credit card management through their ezcardinfo.com portal.

Garfield County Bank distinguishes itself through its commitment to local community service and financial education. The bank operates a free Financial Education Center offering interactive learning resources on topics including credit scores, mortgages, and identity theft protection. This educational focus reflects their philosophy that financial decisions made today impact long-term life outcomes. As a smaller, locally-owned institution, they emphasize personalized service and deep community integration rather than competing on national scale.

The bank is FDIC-insured, providing deposit protection backed by the U.S. Government—a critical safety feature for consumer deposits. However, as a small regional bank, their product offerings and service capabilities are more limited compared to larger national institutions. Prospective customers should verify specific loan terms, interest rates, and fee structures, which are not detailed on the public website. The bank's geographic focus on Montana means it may have limited digital or remote banking options compared to national neobanks.
services: Credit card products with online access via ezcardinfo.com
Mobile banking app with mobile deposit
ATM access at two locations (drive-up and walk-up)
Free Financial Education Center with interactive learning modules
Full-service branch banking (Redwater Valley Bank in Circle)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Free Financial Education Center with interactive learning on credit, mortgages, and identity theft
Mobile banking app with mobile deposit functionality for iPhone and Android
Dual branch locations (Jordan and Circle) with extended ATM/depository access
Locally-owned institution serving the community since 1960 with established reputation
After-hours debit card support available at 1-866-664-9364
Full-service banking including mortgages, loans, and credit cards
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Montana and Garfield County region
Specific interest rates, fees, and loan terms not disclosed on public website
Smaller institution may have fewer digital banking features than national banks
No mention of business banking products or business loan specifics
Limited online presence compared to larger competitor banks
best_for: Montana residents in or near Garfield County seeking community-focused banking
Customers prioritizing FDIC safety and local relationship banking over large-scale convenience
Individuals interested in financial education resources and personalized service
Families with long-term ties to the Garfield County area
diagnosis: Garfield County Bank is best for Montana residents in Garfield County and surrounding areas who value local, personalized banking relationships and FDIC protection. The primary caveat is that specific product terms, interest rates, and fees are not publicly disclosed on their website, requiring direct contact with the bank for detailed pricing and feature information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
